commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] There is a narrow mechanical repair to remove the changelog entry, but merge still needs maintainer/security review and a clean rebase.; Needs attention: The patch has no supply-chain concern, but it intentionally suppresses a security audit warning in more cases and needs owner review before merge.; Review finding: [P2] Drop the changelog entry — CHANGELOG.md:35 (sha=7ef79240aaf1af9a23fcb66ccb7f709927a98fc2)
commands.review_summary:
The PR refines the security audit multi-user heuristic so sandboxed group/channel ingress and routed WhatsApp groups do not trigger the personal-assistant warning, with docs and regression tests.

PR surface: Source +356, Tests +146, Docs +1. Total +503 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source at medium confidence: current main flags configured group targets and treats `sandbox.mode="non-main"` as unguarded in the audit heuristic, matching the false-positive path described by the linked issue. I did not run the audit command because this review is read-only and avoids commands that may create artifacts.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] There is a narrow mechanical repair to remove the changelog entry, but merge still needs maintainer/security review and a clean rebase.

target.title: fix(security): respect sandboxed group ingress
target.body:
## Summary
- Evaluate shared-user ingress against the agents/routes that can actually receive that ingress, including explicit group bindings and WhatsApp broadcast fan-out.
- Treat `sandbox.mode="non-main"` as guarding external group/channel/DM ingress, while keeping local main-session exposure checks intact.
- Remove the lint-only no-op type conversion that was failing `check-lint` on the PR branch.
- Update security audit docs and changelog for the refined heuristic.

Closes #27306.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Allow a personal WhatsApp DM/main session to run unsandboxed while an allowlisted WhatsApp group routes to a sandboxed agent, without emitting `security.trust_model.multi_user_heuristic`.
- **Real environment tested:** Windows local checkout, Node v24.14.0 via the bundled Codex runtime for the live audit proof, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`; branch refreshed at `0122574521` after rebasing onto `upstream/main`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Created a temporary JSON5 config with `agents.list.main.sandbox.mode="off"`, `agents.list.friends.sandbox.mode="all"`, a WhatsApp `groupPolicy="allowlist"` target, and a `bindings[]` group route to `friends`; then ran `node scripts/run-node.mjs security audit --json`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Copied console output from the post-patch audit run:
```json
{
  "findingCount": 3,
  "hasMultiUserHeuristic": false,
  "securityFindings": []
}
```
- **Observed result after fix:** `security.trust_model.multi_user_heuristic` is absent for the sandboxed group route, while the regression test still verifies unguarded shared ingress is warned.
- **What was not tested:** No live WhatsApp gateway was connected; this patch changes config/audit behavior only. Security-labeled semantics still need maintainer/security review.

## Verification
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md docs/cli/security.md docs/gateway/security/audit-checks.md src/security/audit-extra.sync.ts src/security/audit-trust-model.test.ts`
- `pnpm lint:core`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/security/audit-trust-model.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm check:changed --base upstream/main`
- `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD`
- Live CLI proof above with bundled Node 24

Note: the local shell Node is v22.15.0, so pnpm emitted the repo engine warning (`>=22.16.0` required). Live CLI proof used the bundled Node v24.14.0 runtime.

## Issue hygiene
Rechecked before commit: #27306 was still open, unassigned, had zero comments, and GitHub search found zero PRs referencing it.
target.author: hemantsudarshan
